---
title: "random_forest"
author: "Alex"
date: "2026-04-10"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(dplyr)
```

# Get cleaned data
```{r}
march_data <- read.csv("clean_march_data.csv")
march_data <- march_data %>% select(-any_of(c("X")))
march_data$POSTSEASON <- factor(march_data$POSTSEASON, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes"))
```

# Split into test/train
```{r}
ratio <- 0.7
set.seed(12345)

train_rows <- sample(1:nrow(march_data), ratio * nrow(march_data))
train <- march_data[train_rows, ]
test <- march_data[-train_rows, ]
```

# Train random forest
TEAM is excluded from predictors — it's just an identifier, not a feature. Random forest handles the CONF factor natively and doesn't need scaling.
```{r}
library(caret)
library(randomForest)

train_x <- train %>% select(-POSTSEASON, -TEAM)
train_y <- train$POSTSEASON

ctrl <- trainControl(method = "cv", number = 5, classProbs = TRUE)

rf_model <- train(
  x = train_x,
  y = train_y,
  method = "rf",
  trControl = ctrl,
  tuneLength = 3
)

rf_model
varImp(rf_model)
```

# Predict tournament likelihood for each test team
```{r}
test_x <- test %>% select(-POSTSEASON, -TEAM)

# type = "prob" returns a probability for each class instead of a hard label
rf_probs <- predict(rf_model, test_x, type = "prob")
prob_yes <- rf_probs[, "Yes"]

# Bucket into tiers: Unlikely (0-30%), Bubble (30-60%), Likely (60-100%)
tier <- cut(
  prob_yes,
  breaks = c(-Inf, 0.30, 0.60, Inf),
  labels = c("Unlikely (0-30%)", "Bubble (30-60%)", "Likely (60-100%)"),
  right = FALSE
)

results <- data.frame(
  TEAM = test$TEAM,
  prob_tournament = round(prob_yes, 3),
  tier = tier,
  actual = test$POSTSEASON
)

# Sort highest probability first
results <- results[order(-results$prob_tournament), ]

# Random forest gives much smoother probabilities than a single C5.0 tree
# since each prediction is averaged across hundreds of trees.

table(results$tier)

results[results$tier == "Bubble (30-60%)", ]
```

# Tier accuracy — how well do the tiers match actual outcomes?
```{r}
# Cross-tab of predicted tier vs actual outcome
tier_matrix <- table(Tier = results$tier, Actual = results$actual)
tier_matrix

# Proportion of teams in each tier that actually made the tournament
tier_accuracy <- prop.table(tier_matrix, margin = 1)
round(tier_accuracy, 3)
```
